title: Indonesia and Russia Continue to Contribute to Majority of China's Coal Imports
section_path: Indonesia and Russia Continue to Contribute to Majority of China's Coal Imports
section_path_text: Indonesia and Russia Continue to Contribute to Majority of China's Coal Imports
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
China's coal import data for August by origin was recently released and showed that imports from Indonesia have remained China's primary source and Russia the second largest source. Imports from Indonesia totaled 15.8 million tons, which is up from July by 4.1 million tons (35%) but down year-on-year by 1.6 million tons (-9%). Image reference: Chart 1 (54).jpg | assets/2022-10-11_indonesia-and-russia-continue-to-contribute-to-majority-of-chinas-coal-imports_img_chart1285429_0fd4978de7ec.jpg
Imports from Russia totaled 8.5 million tons, which is up from July by 1.1 million tons (15%) and up year-on-year by 3.1 million tons (57%).
